name: Jack Sheldon
abstract: Beryl Cyril "Jack" Sheldon Jr. (November 30, 1931 – December 27, 2019) was an American jazz trumpeter, singer, and actor.

interviews.title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

concerts.label: Benny Goodman 40th Anniversary Concert at Carnegie Hall

quotes.text: Yeah. I’ve been there. What was the last time, oh yeah Carnegie Hall with Rosemary Clooney, yeah that was a thrill, God I loved her. And I’ve done Carnegie Hall with Benny a few times, we did the 40 year anniversary, and then I did a few concerts with Benny back then, and I got to go back with Rosemary Clooney, we did a tribute to Bing Crosby. I love here, she’s a great artist.
quotes.caption: Jack Sheldon recalls performing at Benny Goodman's 40th Anniversary Concert
quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I had met Jack Sheldon, who was a trumpet player at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: But when he worked with me he knew that he could not do that. ... We played at the Carnation Hall over at Disneyland. And I hired Jack, because Jack had a good following. And I always featured Jack at the end of the set before I played my ballad
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: You know when Jack Sheldon came on the band... Med Flory would get up and sing, Ray Simms would play and sing and Jack calls out a tune
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: He hired Red Norvo’s quintet... and added Jack Sheldon and Bill Harris and Flip Phillips... And we toured Europe and had a great time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: Jack came and sat down next to me and we did the session and everything was beautiful.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: He did a great deal, we did a lot of work together and had an awful lot of laughs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
